articles.title: 9 students brought to hospital after school bus crash in Everett
articles.description:
Of the 11 students on the bus, nine were transported to area hospitals “for observation,” officials said.

The post 9 students brought to hospital after school bus crash in Everett appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Nine of 11 students on a school bus were hospitalized after a crash in Everett.
Officials reported the students were taken to area hospitals for observation.
Local parents express concern over school bus safety following the incident.

articles.title: Huge endangered snapping turtle, somehow loose in Mass., rescued from pond
articles.description:
“This is one of the first alligator snappers that's been found in the wild in Massachusetts, which means that somebody had it as a pet,” a herpetologist with MassWildlife said.

The post Huge endangered snapping turtle, somehow loose in Mass., rescued from pond appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Endangered alligator snapping turtle rescued from a pond in Massachusetts.
MassWildlife confirms it's one of the first found in the wild here.
Residents urged to report exotic pets to prevent wildlife disruption.

articles.title: 2-time NASCAR champ Kyle Busch dies at 41 after being hospitalized with a ‘severe illness’
articles.description:
The Busch Family, Richard Childress Racing, and NASCAR issued a joint statement Thursday saying Busch died after being hospitalized. No cause of death was given.

The post 2-time NASCAR champ Kyle Busch dies at 41 after being hospitalized with a ‘severe illness’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch died at 41 after hospitalization for a severe illness.
Busch's death was confirmed by the Busch Family and Richard Childress Racing on May 21.
His passing may impact local NASCAR fans and events in the Boston area.

articles.title: After state trooper killed, Senate approves amendment aimed at deterring wrong-way drivers
articles.description:
The Senate voted 40-0 to require new infrastructure and multi-sensory warning devices to address wrong-way drivers.

The post After state trooper killed, Senate approves amendment aimed at deterring wrong-way drivers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senate approved a 40-0 amendment to deter wrong-way drivers in Massachusetts.
New infrastructure and multi-sensory warning devices will be implemented to enhance safety.
This initiative aims to prevent future accidents and protect Boston residents.

articles.title: Thoughts on owning one gas and one electric vehicle
articles.description:
John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, hears from a reader considering replacing one of their family’s two gas vehicles with an electric.

The post Thoughts on owning one gas and one electric vehicle appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: John Paul advises Boston families on balancing gas and electric vehicle ownership.
AAA Northeast's Car Doctor addresses reader concerns about vehicle replacement options.
Transitioning to electric vehicles could reduce Boston's carbon footprint and fuel costs.

articles.title: Red Sox top prospect Franklin Arias makes major leap in latest ESPN rankings
articles.description:
"Arias is still 20 years old, so there’s potential for 20- to 25-homer upside if he keeps this up and the elements click together in the coming years.”

The post Red Sox top prospect Franklin Arias makes major leap in latest ESPN rankings appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Franklin Arias, Red Sox top prospect, significantly rises in ESPN rankings.
At just 20 years old, Arias shows potential for 20-25 home runs.
His development could enhance Red Sox's future performance for Boston fans.

articles.title: Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind
articles.description:
“After review, the matter is being handled internally, and no criminal charges will be filed,” Watertown police said.

The post Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Watertown police responded to a threat against Perkins School for the Blind.
Police confirmed no criminal charges will be filed after internal review.
Local residents can feel reassured as the situation is under control.

articles.title: Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut
articles.description:
The second suspect was allegedly also involved in the sexual assault of a Connecticut woman’s dog, discovered by police in April.

The post Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Second suspect arrested in connection with dog sexual abuse case in Connecticut.
Incident involving a woman's dog reported to police in April 2023.
Boston residents urged to report animal abuse to prevent similar incidents.
